diagnosis: Three orphaned worktrees were preserved under orphaned-worktrees/ but claims/results/blocked ledgers were empty. Retro-claims and blocked decisions now close the protocol before new fan-out.
recommendation: ship

diagnosis: Low-quality OUP fallback snippets were counted as abstracts on gold-empty rows; filter citation/PDF-only/section-editor chrome.
recommendation: ship

diagnosis: Best measured Taylor candidate improves metric by using truncated dc.Description snippets but risks degrading production full abstracts.
recommendation: block_pending_referee

diagnosis: Dominant miss variant is modern ScienceDirect AuthorGroups JSON affiliations; no parser patch implemented yet.
recommendation: continue_diagnosis

diagnosis: Modern ScienceDirect pages expose aff1/aff2 AuthorGroups data in script[type=application/json]; parser now reads that source and enriches DOM authors.
recommendation: ship_and_run_full10k_batch

diagnosis: Broad ScienceDirect script[type=application/json] author-affiliation fallback lifted a focused slice but regressed full current-Goldie affiliations/corresponding. Disable it and route app-JSON-only values to Goldie-backfilled Browserbase grounding.
recommendation: ship_regression_rollback_then_ground_backfilled_candidates

diagnosis: Browserbase-grounded IEEE affiliation candidates were audited as derived-corpus candidates. 95/100 current DOI+field rows have sufficient candidate_text_match evidence; 5 remain blocked for manual review or failed/page-identity-only grounding.
recommendation: continue_grounding_remaining_ieee_affiliations

diagnosis: Taylor eBook/chapter pages on taylorfrancis.com were not routed to the Taylor parser and publisher-specific no-affiliation output was dropped before fallback. JSON-LD authors and product JSON abstracts now parse; dispatcher preserves publisher-specific author/abstract content after affiliated paths. Apparent IEEE author aggregate regression is gold-empty/parser-present backfill accounting, not a non-empty-gold parser mismatch.
recommendation: ship_and_continue_goldie_backfilled_grounding

diagnosis: Browserbase smoke grounded 10 Goldie-backfilled candidates across Elsevier pdf_url, IEEE authors, and Lippincott abstract. Candidate-text evidence supports 7 derived candidates; 3 Elsevier PDF rows were blocked because the rendered landing page proved DOI identity but not the candidate PDF URL.
recommendation: scale authors/abstract backfill; patch PDF grounding before scaling Elsevier pdf_url

diagnosis: Patched PDF candidate verifier navigated the proposed Elsevier /pdf URLs. All three sample URLs returned HTTP 200 but resolved to ScienceDirect abstract pages, so they are not approved as PDF labels.
recommendation: Do not scale Elsevier pdf_url backfill on /pdf candidates until a candidate URL resolves to /pdf, pdfft, .pdf, citation_pdf_url, or a visible PDF link.

diagnosis: Browserbase-grounded IEEE authors batch resolved n_authors-only candidates from rendered IEEE authorNames when counts matched. All 25 rows have candidate evidence and are approved into a derived ledger.
recommendation: Ground remaining IEEE author candidates in 25-50 row batches; keep Referee approval separate from current-Goldie KPI.

diagnosis: Modern ScienceDirect puts explicit Correspondence author icon SVGs in separate icon-only sibling buttons after the author name. The parser now maps those sibling icons to the preceding surname-bearing author before the next author tag.
recommendation: ship_scoped_gate_and_run_next_full10k_batch

diagnosis: Full-10K control gate confirms the modern ScienceDirect sibling corresponding-icon parser fix raises global corresponding accuracy without moving other fields. Elsevier corresponding moves from 0.926845 to 0.934786; overall corresponding moves from 0.961051 to 0.963219.
recommendation: shipped_full10k_measured_continue_next_higher_priority_field_or_goldie_backfilled_grounding

recommendation: Ground remaining 188 IEEE author candidates in 25-50 row batches; keep derived-corpus metrics separate from current-Goldie KPI.
